questions.text: Which gas is necessary for combustion?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is necessary for combustion. It supports burning and is called the supporter of combustion.

questions.text: Water can be used to extinguish oil fires.
questions.explanation: Water should NOT be used on oil fires because oil floats on water and spreads the fire. Use foam or CO₂ extinguishers.

questions.correctAnswer: The minimum temperature at which a substance catches fire.
questions.explanation: Ignition temperature is the lowest temperature at which a substance will spontaneously ignite and burn without external heat source.

questions.text: The innermost zone of a flame is the ________ zone.
questions.explanation: The innermost zone of a candle flame is the darkest zone containing unburnt wax vapours.
